clean_story:
Now I will teach you planets.

Look up when sun sleeps—tiny fires move slow like lazy animals. Not fire, not sun, not blinking like bird-eyes in dark. Some bright like wet stone in moonlight, some red like old blood on rock. They do not fall, do not burn earth, do not make sound—just slide, cold and far, like silent fish in black water. Touch? No. Taste? No. But if you watch long, they walk the same path, never rushing like scared beast, never hiding like shy plant.
